cohesion_notes: Nagelsmann had been in charge for under a year but the spring performances were improving.
quality_notes: Musiala Wirtz Havertz and Fullkrug offered elite variety while Rudiger Tah and Neuer formed a strong defensive core.

cohesion_notes: Clarke retained a settled tactical core.
depth_notes: Several injuries left limited alternatives at full-back midfield and striker.
quality_notes: McTominay and Robertson supplied quality but the forward line lacked a proven high-level scorer.
dropout_notes: Dykes Ferguson Hickey and Patterson were unavailable.
injury_notes: Key players entered camp with recent fitness disruption.

cohesion_notes: Rossi had a settled structure and established core.
quality_notes: Szoboszlai and Sallai gave Hungary strong transition and set-piece threat.

cohesion_notes: Yakin retained an experienced tournament core.
quality_notes: The attack was solid rather than prolific while Akanji Xhaka and Sommer anchored a strong defensive structure.
injury_notes: Embolo and Zakaria entered the tournament short of full match fitness.

cohesion_notes: De la Fuente had established a clear possession and pressing structure.
depth_notes: Elite options remained across midfield and attack.
quality_notes: Yamal Williams Rodri and Pedri provided elite creation with a technically strong defence.

age_notes: Modric Perisic Brozovic and several starters formed an aging core.
cohesion_notes: Dalic retained the experienced 2018 and 2022 tournament spine.
quality_notes: Croatia had elite midfield control but less pace and finishing depth than the leading favourites.

cohesion_notes: Spalletti was still reshaping the European champions after taking over in 2023.
quality_notes: Italy lacked a dominant scorer but retained strong midfield and defensive quality.
dropout_notes: Acerbi and Scalvini were ruled out before the tournament.
injury_notes: Defensive injuries forced late changes to the selected squad.

cohesion_notes: Sylvinho had built a disciplined and settled qualifying side.
depth_notes: The squad had limited proven depth at elite international level.
quality_notes: Broja Asllani and Djimsiti were the main high-level performers in an otherwise modest squad.

cohesion_notes: Kek had a settled compact system.
depth_notes: There was limited depth beyond the main starting eleven.
quality_notes: Sesko offered high-upside attacking threat while Oblak was an elite goalkeeper.

cohesion_notes: Hjulmand retained the established Euro 2020 tactical core.
quality_notes: Hojlund Eriksen and Wind gave varied attacking options with Andersen Christensen and Schmeichel behind them.

cohesion_notes: The talented squad had struggled for consistent balance under Stojkovic.
quality_notes: Vlahovic Mitrovic and Tadic offered major attacking quality but the defensive unit was less convincing.

cohesion_notes: Southgate retained a highly experienced tournament structure.
depth_notes: The squad had elite alternatives in most attacking and midfield positions.
quality_notes: Kane Bellingham Saka Foden and Palmer formed an elite attack while defence was strong but less settled.
dropout_notes: Maguire missed the tournament after failing to recover in time.
injury_notes: Shaw entered the tournament without recent match fitness.

cohesion_notes: Probierz had taken charge during qualifying and was still adapting the system.
depth_notes: Attacking alternatives were reduced by injuries.
quality_notes: Lewandowski remained elite but Poland had limited creation and defensive quality around him.
dropout_notes: Milik was ruled out immediately before the tournament.
injury_notes: Lewandowski suffered a thigh injury and was unavailable for the opening match.

cohesion_notes: Koeman had an established system and experienced core.
quality_notes: Gakpo Depay and Simons offered strong attacking options with Van Dijk Ake and an elite defensive group.
dropout_notes: De Jong Koopmeiners and Timber were unavailable.
injury_notes: Midfield injuries materially weakened the selected squad.

cohesion_notes: Rangnick had established one of the strongest pressing structures in qualifying.
depth_notes: Several injuries reduced depth despite a coherent first eleven.
quality_notes: Sabitzer Baumgartner and a coordinated press created danger but defensive absences reduced the ceiling.
dropout_notes: Alaba Schlager and Kalajdzic were among the major absentees.
injury_notes: Several selected players had recent fitness concerns.

cohesion_notes: Deschamps retained an experienced World Cup finalist core.
depth_notes: Elite alternatives were available in every unit.
quality_notes: Mbappe Griezmann Dembele and Thuram led an elite attack with Saliba Upamecano Hernandez and Maignan behind them.

age_notes: De Bruyne Vertonghen and several core players were in the later stage of their careers.
cohesion_notes: Tedesco was integrating a younger generation around the remaining senior core.
quality_notes: De Bruyne Doku Lukaku and Trossard gave Belgium an elite attack but the defence was no longer at its previous level.
dropout_notes: Courtois was not selected after his long injury and dispute with the coach.

cohesion_notes: Calzona had built a disciplined and settled qualifying team.
depth_notes: The squad had limited attacking depth.
quality_notes: Lobotka and Skriniar were high-level anchors but scoring options were modest.

cohesion_notes: Iordanescu had developed a cohesive unbeaten qualifying side.
depth_notes: Depth at top European club level was limited.
quality_notes: The team was organised and athletic but lacked elite attacking creation.

cohesion_notes: Rebrov had a settled core with experience across leading European leagues.
quality_notes: Dovbyk Mudryk Tsygankov and Sudakov offered strong attack while the defence was closer to average.

age_notes: A young squad included several players with limited major-tournament experience.
cohesion_notes: Montella had taken over late in qualifying and the side remained tactically volatile.
quality_notes: Calhanoglu Guler Yildiz and Akturkoglu offered major technical upside but defence was less reliable.
dropout_notes: Kabak was ruled out after a knee injury.
injury_notes: Several defenders entered the tournament with recent injury disruption.

cohesion_notes: Sagnol retained the settled core that won the play-off.
depth_notes: This was the narrowest squad among the tournament debutants.
quality_notes: Kvaratskhelia and Mamardashvili were elite talents but quality elsewhere was limited.

age_notes: Ronaldo and Pepe were 39 and 41 although the wider squad had a balanced age profile.
cohesion_notes: Martinez had established the system during a dominant qualifying campaign.
depth_notes: Elite alternatives were available across attack midfield and defence.
quality_notes: Ronaldo Leao Fernandes Silva and Jota gave Portugal elite scoring and creation with a strong defensive group.

cohesion_notes: Hasek had only taken charge in January 2024.
quality_notes: Schick Hlozek and Soucek gave Czechia direct attacking and set-piece threat with an average defence.
